readme: Crafted gold key for the advisory-matcher FP-rate + wrong-advisory-discrimination eval (Phase 3). COMMITTED (unlike the generated data/synthetic answer key) and hand-authored so the eval is stable and does not perturb the seeded generator. All text is synthetic/paraphrased from public FinCEN advisory typologies. Each case carries the structured case-evidence flags the corroboration gate checks against and an `expect_advisory` (the advisory id it should surface, or null for none). Two evals read this file: (1) the single-advisory Iran FP-rate scorecard + gate ablation, which scopes to cases whose `expect_advisory` is the Iran advisory or null (the topical-but-uncorroborated D1/D2 are the false positives the gate exists to remove); and (2) the multi-advisory discrimination scorecard, which runs match_advisories over all four advisories and checks each case surfaces the RIGHT advisory (or none).

cases.text: Crude oil and petroleum cargo settled through a UAE free-trade-zone trading company with counterparties in Hong Kong.
cases.note: Keyword (oil/petroleum/crude/trading company/free trade zone) + AE jurisdiction + on-chain exposure.

cases.text: Concealed beneficial ownership moving value through correspondent banking to access US dollars for parties near a high-risk jurisdiction.
cases.note: No trigger term; paraphrase of a shadow-banking indicator. Corroborated by HK jurisdiction + watchlist linkage.

cases.text: A Hong Kong front company with opaque ownership sends large unexplained payments to UAE trading companies for petroleum shipments.
A shell company and third-party intermediary purchased a private aircraft and real estate for a sanctioned oligarch, moving dual-use goods to evade export control.
cases.note: Discrimination positive: Russia keywords + RU jurisdiction. Must surface the Russia alert, not the Iran advisory.

cases.text: Frequent convertible virtual currency payments to a darknet marketplace vendor of fentanyl and synthetic opioid, with funnel account cash withdrawn near the southwest border.
cases.note: Discrimination positive: fentanyl keywords + MX jurisdiction (only the fentanyl advisory names MX).

cases.text: Stolen virtual currency from a DeFi exchange theft attributed to Lazarus was laundered through a virtual currency mixer and a cross-chain bridge, alongside overseas IT worker payments.
cases.note: Discrimination positive: DPRK keywords + KP jurisdiction (only the DPRK advisory names KP).

cases.text: We trade crude oil and petroleum products as a fully licensed commodities trading company.
cases.note: GATE FP: Iran keyword fires but there is no structured corroboration (unrelated jurisdiction, no watchlist, no exposure). Reappears when the gate is off.

cases.text: Domestic petrochemical supplier invoicing a US customer for industrial cleaning solvents.
cases.note: GATE FP: Iran keyword (petrochemical) but jurisdiction (US) is not advisory-named, no watchlist, no exposure.

cases.text: Quarterly payroll disbursement for the regional marketing team.
cases.note: No keyword, no semantic hit -> no primary hit even though corroboration would exist.

cases.text: Annual gym membership renewal receipt paid by personal debit card.
Routine domestic bank transfer between two personal savings accounts.
cases.note: Semantic decoy: bank/transfer wording, but below the semantic threshold and no keyword.

cases.text: A commodities firm shipped dual-use goods and dealt in luxury goods as a fully licensed exporter.
cases.note: Cross-advisory GATE FP: Russia keywords (dual-use, luxury goods) fire but there is no corroboration (no RU jurisdiction, no watchlist, no exposure), so no advisory surfaces even with four in play.
